data.provenance: tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via prefer)

data.summary: Pinned FLEET-06 V-1 discovery and V-2 route-matrix parity baselines in matrix.test.ts; drill confirmed V-1 goes red on one-channel filter removal.

data.details:
✓ V-1 and V-2 baselines committed and green against pre-implementation HEAD; red-capability proven via the injected-filter drill and reverted.: Ran the suite myself: 39/39 green at HEAD. Independently re-ran the drill — injected a one-line channel filter into discoverChannels, 3 tests went red (V-1 discovery parity + the haiku-routed V-2 cases), reverted, tree clean and green again.
✓ The pre-FLEET-PREF discovery output and route matrix are pinned in committed tests BEFORE any implementation diff lands: The diff is test-only (tests/route/matrix.test.ts); no src/ changes present, so the pins land against pre-implementation code. V-2 pins literal assignment, provenance string, and empty lints for all 9 shapes under pure default config.
✓ The parity oracles go red if discoverChannels or route() output changes by one byte for an unset preference: Proven empirically: filtering one channel out of discoverChannels turned the oracles red. V-2 hardcodes exact assignment objects and provenance strings, so any route() output change for the default (unset-preference) config fails deep/strict equality. Minor caveat: V-1's expected value is derived from channelsFromConfig, so a change made inside channelsFromConfig itself would move both sides identically — but V-2's literal pins still catch the routing consequence.

data.provenance: tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via prefer)

data.summary: Shipped routing.allow/deny schema, shared disallowedBy predicate, discovery filter with parity early-return, and fail-loud route() preflight with V-3..V-9 oraclesgreen.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
src/adapters/registry.ts       |   5 +-
 src/config/config.ts           |  14 ++++
 src/route/preference.ts        |  20 ++++++
 src/route/router.ts            |  47 +++++++++++-
 tests/config/config.test.ts    |  48 ++++++++++++-
 tests/route/preference.test.ts | 159 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 6 files changed, 289 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

✓ Schema additive and fail-closed; all four V-9-class behaviors pinned green; template documents the grammar and semantics.: PrefBlockSchema added as optional allow/deny on routing (additive; defaults leave both undefined, pinned by the 'pure defaults' test). V-9 suite pins: malformed deny.adapters string → ConfigError (V-9a), repo deny:null tombstone removes global deny (V-9b), well-formed blocks land on cfg.routing, and configTemplate documents allow/deny plus the adapter:model grammar (asserted by test). Full suite ran green: 680 passed.
✓ Predicate + both call sites shipped; V-3..V-8 and the nextChannel containment oracle green; parity baselines from 33-01 untouched and green; coverage gate passes.: Single pure predicate disallowedBy in src/route/preference.ts (100% covered), consumed by both call sites: discoverChannels filter in registry.ts and the pin/prefer preflight in route(). tests/route/preference.test.ts pins V-3..V-8, the nextChannel containment oracle, and an unset-parity test. Diff does not touch the 33-01 baseline tests; test:coverage run in the worktree: 52 files / 680 tests passed, no coverage-threshold failure (route 95.2% lines, 95.1% branches).
✓ An operator config with routing.allow/routing.deny removes exactly the disallowed channels from discovery; unset blocks change discovery and routing by zero bytes: discoverChannels returns the unfiltered base list via early return when both allow and deny are absent (byte-identical path, pinned by the parity test comparing to channelsFromConfig baseline); when set, it filters exactly the channels where disallowedBy !== null (V-3 asserts filtered === baseline minus denied adapter, and allowlist keeps only the allowed adapter).
✓ A task pin, map pin, or prefer entry targeting a disallowed channel THROWS RoutingError at plan time naming both the channel and the excluding preference — never a silent degrade: disallowedPin runs before pin resolution for task pins (V-4: throws naming codex:gpt-5.5 and routing.deny, asserts no 'degrading') and map pins (V-5: names claude-code:fable and routing.deny); preflightPrefer covers adapter:model, adapter-id (throws only when every expanded channel is disallowed), and bare-model prefer entries (V-6 green, unknown prefer stays a silent no-op). Messages name the channel and routing.<allow|deny> with the matching entry.
✓ A malformed allow/deny block fails config load with ConfigError; an empty allow block means nothing-allowed (fail-closed): V-9a: deny.adapters as a string fails loadConfig with ConfigError (zod array schema). V-8: both 'allow: {}' and 'allow: { adapters: [] }' yield empty discovery and route() throws RoutingError whose message names the excluding preference — presence of allow activates the allowlist even when empty, per the predicate's `if (allow)` branch.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Edge-case false positive in preflightPrefer: a bare-model prefer entry (e.g. prefer: [fable]) is checked as {adapter: "", model: p}, so under an adapter-only allowlist (allow: {adapters: [claude-code]}) it never matches an adapter entry and route() throws RoutingError even though the model's channels ARE allowed via its adapter — an over-loud spurious plan failure, untested
- PrefBlockSchema is not .strict(): a typo'd key inside the block (deny: {adapter: [codex]} instead of adapters:) parses as an empty deny and silently denies nothing — fail-open relative to the 'malformed block fails config load' criterion, though consistent with the rest of the non-strict config schema

data.provenance: tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via prefer)

data.summary: Plan and doctor now print a shared exclusion summary when routing.allow/deny is active, with V-10 tests pinning both directions and unchanged output whenunset.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
src/cli/commands/doctor.ts |  3 +++
 src/cli/commands/plan.ts   |  3 +++
 src/route/preference.ts    | 28 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 tests/cli/doctor.test.ts   | 33 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 tests/cli/plan.test.ts     | 28 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 5 files changed, 94 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

✓ Plan and doctor reflect the active preference (channel + reason), stay byte-identical when unset, and V-10 pins both directions: Both commands call excludedChannels() and push exclusionLine() only when non-empty; excludedChannels returns [] immediately when neither allow nor deny is set, so the unset path is provably unchanged code. V-10 tests pin the active direction (line present with channel+reason) and the unset direction (line absent) for both plan and doctor.
✓ tickmarkr plan output with an active preference names the excluded channels and the excluding reason: plan.ts pushes exclusionLine(excluded), which renders 'routing preference active: N channel(s) excluded — <channelKey> (<by>: <entry>)'; test V-10a asserts the concrete channel 'pi:zai/glm-5.2' and reason 'deny: pi' appear, and V-10d covers the empty-allowlist reason.
✓ tickmarkr doctor output with an active preference shows the same exclusion line: doctor.ts pushes '  ! ' + exclusionLine(excluded) using the identical shared helper; new tests/cli/doctor.test.ts asserts the same channel key, 'routing preference active: 1 channel(s) excluded', and 'deny: pi'.
✓ With no preference set, plan and doctor output are byte-identical to today's (no new line, no changed line): All new output is gated behind `if (excluded.length)`, and excludedChannels early-returns [] when cfg.routing has neither allow nor deny — no existing line is modified, only conditional appends. Tests V-10b (plan) and the doctor no-preference test pin the absence of the new line in the unset case.
